infobox.value: (Spanish pronunciation: [[floˌɾiðaˈβlaŋka]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Spanish "Help:IPA/Spanish"), locally also simply **Florida**) is a [municipality](/wiki/Municipality "Municipality") in the department of [Santander](/wiki/Santander_Department "Santander Department") situated at an altitude of 925 metres (3,035 ft) in the [Eastern Ranges](/wiki/Cordillera_Oriental_(Colombia) "Cordillera Oriental (Colombia)") of the Colombian [Andes](/wiki/Andes "Andes"). It is part of the metropolitan area of the departmental capital [Bucaramanga](/wiki/Bucaramanga "Bucaramanga"). Floridablanca is known for its parks and the Piedra del Sol, a large rock with spirals and circles carved by the [Guane](/wiki/Guane_people "Guane people") over 1,000 years ago.[[2]](#cite_note-VivaFlorida-2) This city is home of the tallest statue of Jesus Christ in Colombia, "El Santisimo". The statue is 40 metres (130 ft) high and weighs 40 tonnes (88,000 lb).[[3]](#cite_note-MinubeStatue-3) The city is situated along the Ruta Nacional 45A, a branch of the [Pan American Highway](/wiki/Pan_American_Highway "Pan American Highway"), connecting the capital [Bogotá](/wiki/Bogot%C3%A1 "Bogotá") with [Santa Marta](/wiki/Santa_Marta "Santa Marta") at the Caribbean coast and [Cúcuta](/wiki/C%C3%BAcuta "Cúcuta") as a gateway to [Venezuela](/wiki/Venezuela "Venezuela").[[4]](#cite_note-TiempoAlegria-4) Together with other neighboring municipalities of Bucaramanga, [Girón](/wiki/Gir%C3%B3n,_Santander "Girón, Santander") and [Piedecuesta](/wiki/Piedecuesta "Piedecuesta"), Floridablanca is one of the growth areas for the capital of Santander with numerous constructions happening in recent years.[[5]](#cite_note-VanguardiaExpansion-5)

**Floridablanca** (Spanish pronunciation: [[floˌɾiðaˈβlaŋka]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Spanish "Help:IPA/Spanish"), locally also simply **Florida**) is a [municipality](/wiki/Municipality "Municipality") in the department of [Santander](/wiki/Santander_Department "Santander Department") situated at an altitude of 925 metres (3,035 ft) in the [Eastern Ranges](/wiki/Cordillera_Oriental_(Colombia) "Cordillera Oriental (Colombia)") of the Colombian [Andes](/wiki/Andes "Andes"). It is part of the metropolitan area of the departmental capital [Bucaramanga](/wiki/Bucaramanga "Bucaramanga"). Floridablanca is known for its parks and the Piedra del Sol, a large rock with spirals and circles carved by the [Guane](/wiki/Guane_people "Guane people") over 1,000 years ago.[[2]](#cite_note-VivaFlorida-2) This city is home of the tallest statue of Jesus Christ in Colombia, "El Santisimo". The statue is 40 metres (130 ft) high and weighs 40 tonnes (88,000 lb).[[3]](#cite_note-MinubeStatue-3) The city is situated along the Ruta Nacional 45A, a branch of the [Pan American Highway](/wiki/Pan_American_Highway "Pan American Highway"), connecting the capital [Bogotá](/wiki/Bogot%C3%A1 "Bogotá") with [Santa Marta](/wiki/Santa_Marta "Santa Marta") at the Caribbean coast and [Cúcuta](/wiki/C%C3%BAcuta "Cúcuta") as a gateway to [Venezuela](/wiki/Venezuela "Venezuela").[[4]](#cite_note-TiempoAlegria-4) Together with other neighboring municipalities of Bucaramanga, [Girón](/wiki/Gir%C3%B3n,_Santander "Girón, Santander") and [Piedecuesta](/wiki/Piedecuesta "Piedecuesta"), Floridablanca is one of the growth areas for the capital of Santander with numerous constructions happening in recent years.[[5]](#cite_note-VanguardiaExpansion-5)

## Geography and climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Floridablanca,_Santander&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Geography and climate")]

Floridablanca is situated at an altitude of about 925 metres (3,035 ft) in the [Eastern Ranges](/wiki/Cordillera_Oriental_(Colombia) "Cordillera Oriental (Colombia)") of the Colombian [Andes](/wiki/Andes "Andes"), though locally the topography varies greatly. To the east of the city the higher mountains of the Andes rise above the city, thrusted upwards by the north–south-trending [Bucaramanga-Santa Marta Fault](/wiki/Bucaramanga-Santa_Marta_Fault "Bucaramanga-Santa Marta Fault"), one of the major megaregional [faults of Colombia](/wiki/Template:Tectonism_in_Colombia "Template:Tectonism in Colombia"). The area is dominated by the [Bucaramanga Nest](/wiki/Bucaramanga_Nest "Bucaramanga Nest"), the second-most seismically active region in the world.[[6]](#cite_note-SaludNido-6) Because of the relatively low altitude, the city enjoys a very favorable climate all year round. Floridablanca's climate is classified as tropical. When compared with winter, the summers have much more rainfall. The climate is classified as [Aw](/wiki/Tropical_savanna_climate "Tropical savanna climate") (tropical savanna climate) by the [Köppen-Geiger system](/wiki/K%C3%B6ppen_climate_classification "Köppen climate classification"). The average annual temperature is 23.6 °C (74.5 °F) in Floridablanca. The rainfall averages 1,055 millimetres (41.5 in) per year.[[7]](#cite_note-ClimateFlorida-7)

## Geology and biology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Floridablanca,_Santander&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geology and biology")]

The surrounding areas of urban Floridablanca are composed of the [Jurassic](/wiki/Jurassic "Jurassic") [Girón and Jordán formations](/wiki/Gir%C3%B3n_Formation "Girón Formation"), to the east of the Bucaramanga-Santa Marta Fault, running north–south in the east of the municipality, the [Precambrian](/wiki/Precambrian "Precambrian") Bucaramanga Gneiss and Early Jurassic La Corcova Quartzmonzonite crop out and the Ruitoque Massif comprises the [Tambor Formation](/w/index.php?title=Tambor_Formation&action=edit&redlink=1 "Tambor Formation (page does not exist)").[[8]](#cite_note-Plancha120-8)

The rural areas surrounding the city are home to the [endemic](/wiki/Endemism "Endemism") [San Gil climbing salamander](/wiki/San_Gil_climbing_salamander "San Gil climbing salamander") (*Bolitoglossa nicefori*).[[9]](#cite_note-IUCNBolitoglossaNicefori-9)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Floridablanca,_Santander&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: History")]

See also: [Spanish conquest of the Chibchan Nations § Guane](/wiki/Spanish_conquest_of_the_Chibchan_Nations#Guane "Spanish conquest of the Chibchan Nations")

Before the [Spanish conquest](/wiki/Spanish_conquest_of_the_Muisca "Spanish conquest of the Muisca"), the area of Floridablanca was populated by the [Guane](/wiki/Guane_people "Guane people"). The Guane were submitted to the rule of the [Spanish Empire](/wiki/Spanish_Empire "Spanish Empire") by [conquistador](/wiki/List_of_conquistadors_in_Colombia "List of conquistadors in Colombia") [Martín Galeano](/wiki/Mart%C3%ADn_Galeano "Martín Galeano") when he founded [Vélez](/wiki/V%C3%A9lez,_Santander "Vélez, Santander") in 1539, until then part of the [Muisca Confederation](/wiki/Muisca_Confederation "Muisca Confederation"). Modern Floridablanca was founded on 7 November 1817 as "Floridablanca de San Juan Nepomuceno" by Javier Guerra de Mier.[[10]](#cite_note-HistoryFloridablanca-10) The church of San Juan Nepomuceno finished construction in 1832.[[11]](#cite_note-ChurchFloridablanca-11)

## Demography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Floridablanca,_Santander&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Demography")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/56/PoblacionFloridablanca.png/500px-PoblacionFloridablanca.png)](/wiki/File:PoblacionFloridablanca.png)

Growth of the city's population from 1964 to 2005

According to official [DANE](/wiki/National_Administrative_Department_of_Statistics "National Administrative Department of Statistics") census, Floridablanca has 262,165 inhabitants, being the second most populated city in Santander. 47,37% are men, while 52,63% are women. 95,6% of the population over 5 years know how to write and read, meaning, illiteracy rate is about 4,4%.

Statistics show that most of the adult people living in the city are from Bucaramanga:

* 7,7% from Floridablanca
* 44% from [Bucaramanga](/wiki/Bucaramanga "Bucaramanga")
* 33% from other cities of [Santander](/wiki/Santander_Department "Santander Department")
* 16% from other departments

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Floridablanca,_Santander&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Economy")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/88/Jesus_Statue%2C_Floridablanca%2C_Santander%2C_Colombia.jpg/250px-Jesus_Statue%2C_Floridablanca%2C_Santander%2C_Colombia.jpg)](/wiki/File:Jesus_Statue,_Floridablanca,_Santander,_Colombia.jpg)

Statue of [Jesus Christ](/wiki/Jesus_Christ "Jesus Christ") overlooking Floridablanca from the east

A study performed in 1998 provided data on the economical activities in Floridablanca, that has grown since those times. Commerce was the most important sector with 39% of the economical make-up, industrial production of clothing was nearly 10% and the food and drinks industry comprised 7% of the economical activities.[[12]](#cite_note-EconomyFloridablanca-12) The production of sweets, and especially the traditional Colombian dessert of [obleas](/wiki/Oblea "Oblea"), has given Floridablanca the name "the sweet municipality of Colombia".[[13]](#cite_note-DulceFlorida-13)

The central part of the city hosts a concentration of malls; Cañaveral, constructed in 1984,[[14]](#cite_note-CanaveralFlorida-14) and Parque Caracolí, inaugurated in 2013.[[15]](#cite_note-CaracoliFlorida-15)

The city, in recent years grown to connect with Bucaramanga, forms the entry point to the [Chicamocha Canyon](/wiki/Chicamocha_Canyon "Chicamocha Canyon"), located at an hour drive from the city, and hosts touristic activities with a [paragliding](/wiki/Paragliding "Paragliding") school on the *Mesa de Ruitoque*, the Ruitoque Massif in the southwest of the city.[[16]](#cite_note-ParapenteFlorida-16) Local buses to tourist hotspots as [San Gil](/wiki/San_Gil "San Gil") and the Chicamocha Canyon leave from a transport station called "Papi Quiero Piña" ("Dad, I want pineapple").[[17]](#cite_note-VanguardiaPapi-17)

The Jardín Botánico Eloy Valenzuela, a [botanical garden](/wiki/Botanical_garden "Botanical garden") in Floridablanca, was once owned by the El Paragüitas tobacco company.[[2]](#cite_note-VivaFlorida-2)

In 2017, the city was designated by the [Ministry of Commerce, Industry and Tourism](/wiki/Ministry_of_Commerce,_Industry_and_Tourism_(Colombia) "Ministry of Commerce, Industry and Tourism (Colombia)") as one of five "sustainable touristic destinations" of Colombia.[[18]](#cite_note-VanguardiaTourism-18)

## Schools

Some of the schools located in Floridablanca are:

* [Colegio Nuevo Cambridge in Spanish](https://web.archive.org/web/20070930163238/http://www.cambridge.edu.co/colegio%20nuevo%20cambridge/transporte.htm)
